component_identification.content: Using the official rulebook text as the primary source, set up Cosmic Encounter by first sorting all components into player materials, shared components, randomizers, and state markers. Verify component names and iconography against the rulebook contents section before anyone chooses a role or starts the first turn. This avoids the most common setup failure: missing or mis-sorted decks/tokens that break later rules interactions. The rulebook references these core pieces repeatedly in setup and play: Petersen abandoning tech 17 acquiring new tech 17 advanced setup 16 alert lamps 11 alien complexity 11 alien powers 11 losing powers 13 “use” of powers 12 zapping powers 12 alien sheet 11 Alliance Phase 8 allies 8 alternate Filch flare 18 artifact cards 12 attack cards 7 attack versus attack 9 attack versus negotiate 9 B bystanders 13 C canceling powers 13 clarifications 18 colonies 6 colony track 13 committing ships Alliance Phase 8 Launch Phase 8 compensation 10 completing tech 17 cosmic quake 18 cosmic tokens 18 D deals 9 successful deals 10 unsuccessful ...

core_game_loop.content: The official rules describe a repeatable turn rhythm for Cosmic Encounter: perform start-of-turn checks, execute the game’s main action structure in the stated order, resolve triggered effects, then complete cleanup/end-step timing before passing play. Teach this as a fixed checklist so new players do not skip mandatory windows. If an interrupt or reaction occurs, resolve it completely, then return to the unresolved step. Rulebook turn-sequence cues include: Petersen abandoning tech 17 acquiring new tech 17 advanced setup 16 alert lamps 11 alien complexity 11 alien powers 11 losing powers 13 “use” of powers 12 zapping powers 12 alien sheet 11 Alliance Phase 8 allies 8 alternate Filch flare 18 artifact cards 12 attack cards 7 attack versus attack 9 attack versus negotiate 9 B bystanders 13 C canceling powers 13 clarifications 18 colonies 6 colony track 13 committing ships Alliance Phase 8 Launch Phase 8 compensation 10 completing tech 17 ...

detailed_rules.content: Detailed play should follow a strict rulebook hierarchy: explicit card/effect text first, scenario-specific instructions second, and baseline turn structure third. In Cosmic Encounter, most disputes come from timing windows, target legality, replacement effects, and whether an action is optional or mandatory. Read 'must', 'may', and 'cannot' literally; these terms define obligations and restrictions. Always verify eligibility before paying costs, then resolve effects sentence by sentence. If an effect changes board state mid-resolution, finish the current instruction chain before opening new optional actions unless the text says otherwise. Important operational clauses extracted from the official rulebook include: Petersen abandoning tech 17 acquiring new tech 17 advanced setup 16 alert lamps 11 alien complexity 11 alien powers 11 losing powers 13 “use” of powers 12 zapping powers 12 alien sheet 11 Alliance Phase 8 allies 8 alternate Filch flare 18 artifact cards 12 attack cards 7 attack versus attack 9 attack versus negotiate 9 B bystanders 13 C canceling powers 13 clarifications 18 colonies 6 colony track 13 committing ships Alliance Phase 8 Launch Phase 8 compensation 10 completing tech 17 cosmic quake 18 cosmic tokens 18 D deals 9 successful deals 10 unsuccessful deals 10 defender rewards 10 defending lost planets 18 defending without ships 18 defense 7 defense wins 10 defensive ally 8 depleting the destiny deck 18 destiny cards 7 Destiny Phase 7 drawing their own color 8 drawing cards as defense 8, 13 drawing encounter cards 13 Revised Edition Credits Producer: Molly Glover Rules Writing and Revision: Adam Baker, Bill Eberle, Greg Olotka, and Peter Olotka Editing: Carsten ...

scoring_and_endgame.content: Scoring and endgame are determined only by printed rulebook conditions. During play, track all score/objective progress continuously so players can identify whether the game ends immediately on trigger or after round completion. When an end trigger occurs, resolve pending mandatory effects in the order defined by the rules, then apply final scoring and tie-breakers exactly as written. beginner_strategy.content: Beginner strategy from the official rules for Cosmic Encounter: (1) Prioritize legal, repeatable value every turn before attempting high-variance combos. (2) Plan around timing windows—many beginner mistakes are not tactical, they are sequencing errors. (3) Preserve flexibility by keeping at least one adaptable action/resource each round, so you can respond to interrupts and shifting board states. (4) Study loss conditions and penalties early; defensive rule awareness prevents avoidable collapses. (5) In the endgame, choose objective-completing actions over cosmetic optimization, because trigger timing usually matters more than one extra short-term gain. These recommendations come directly from the turn structure and win-condition logic in the official rulebook text.
